title: Pan-Seared Canadian Chops
description: A delicious and flavorful dish made with tender Canadian pork chops. The sour notes from the marinade add a nice balance to the rich flavor of the chops.
instructions: Season the pork chops with salt and pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a large oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the pork chops for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until browned.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and cook for 12-15 minutes, or until the pork chops reach an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C).
While the pork chops are cooking, mix together the brown sugar and apple cider vinegar in a small bowl.
After the pork chops have finished cooking, brush the brown sugar mixture onto both sides of the pork chops.
Let the pork chops rest for 5-10 minutes before serving.
